LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT UZI ALOUSH, IPIL VENTURES, INC., AND ILEVERAGE GROUP, LLC MOTION TO DISQUALIFY PLAINTIFFS' COUNSEL. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED AND AMENDED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. INSUFFICIENT EVIDENCE OF ANY ATTORNEY-CLIENT RELATIONSHIP. FURTHERMORE, DEFENDANT FAILS TO SHOW THAT THE ANTECEDENT RELATIONSHIP WAS SUBSTANTIALLY RELATED TO THE INSTANT ACTION. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For An Order To Compel Further Response To Inspection Demand And Sanctions
Off Calendar Jul-30-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearance:
Wayne Lewis Lesser, Esq., for Plaintiff and moving party: Rosenfeld Law Corporation
Mark Hooshmand, Esq., for Defendant and opposing party: Uzi Aloush

After hearing, tentative ruling is adopted as modified.
Plaintiff Rosenfeld Law Corporation's Motion For An Order To Compel Further Response To Inspection Demand; And For Sanctions [Against Defendant Uzi Aloush] granted in part:
a. Defendant Uzi Aloush shall serve verified responses to plaintiff's inspection demand within five days of notice of entry of this order;
b. Request for sanctions Granted in the amount of $190.00 payable within 30 days.
c. This is order is without prejudice to a motion to compel further responses to specific requests, compliant with Rule of Court 3.1345.

Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Uzi Aloush, Ipil Ventures, Inc., And Ileverage Group, Llc To Disqualify Pltfs' Counsel; Request For Judicial Notice
Off Calendar Jul-16-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT Uzi Aloush, Ipil Ventures, Inc., and Ileverage Group, LLC Motion to Disqualify Plaintiffs' Counsel. Appearances: Wayne Lewis Lesser, Attorney at Law, (415) 563-2111, appearing for Plaintiffs and opposing party; Mark Hooshmand, Esq. Attorney at Law, (415) 835-5900, appearing for Defendants moving party. Argued and the Court adopted and amended its tentative ruling as follows: Motion is denied without prejudice. Insufficient evidence of any attorney-client relationship. Furthermore, defendant fails to show that the antecedent relationship was substantially related to the instant action. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797
